continents.name: The Ring of Seats
continents.biome: A white marble plateau in perfect circle. 12 stone seats at the perimeter. The Seal at center.

places.name: Seat of K-I
places.blurb: The First Architect's seat. Occupant has not risen in cosmic ages.

places.name: Seat of K-V
places.blurb: The current elder's seat. Naming K-V is forbidden.

places.name: The Unwritten Seat
places.blurb: Where K-XII sat. The chair remains because it cannot be removed.

places.name: The Architects' Seal
places.blurb: A gold disk of cork at the ring's center. Keeps VOID-II closed to the outer universe. Has never opened.

places.name: Ferdinand's Seal Cork
places.blurb: The single physical object in VOID-II besides the seats. Keeps Null from leaking in. Regrows between cosmic-age tests.

continents.blurb: The entire ring. The seats, the Seal, and the space between. VOID-II has no other geography.

continents.name: The Library Beyond
continents.biome: A corridor extending infinitely in all six cardinal directions from the Ring.

places.name: The Spine Corridor
places.blurb: The main access to the Library. Every book ever written — and unwritten — is archived here.

places.name: The Unwritten Wing
places.blurb: A section of shelves containing books that were written and then UNWRITTEN. Reading any of them causes the reader to forget they read anything.

places.name: The First Book
places.blurb: A single cork-bound volume on a central pedestal. Ferdinand is said to have grown from a page of this book. The book is unsigned.

places.name: The K-XII Shelf
places.blurb: The shelf that held K-XII's writings. Empty now. A faint outline of missing books remains in the dust.

continents.blurb: Where the Architects' work is stored. Infinite in every direction. The Ledger Librarians tend it in silent shifts.

continents.name: The Butterfly Garden
continents.biome: A small walled courtyard off the Ring. Pale gravel. Seven stone benches. White butterflies.

places.name: The Butterfly Wall
places.blurb: A white wall that butterflies land on when they are about to 'finalize' a decision in the multiverse. The wall records the decision.

places.name: The Silent Bench
places.blurb: A stone bench where K-I sometimes sits. Visitors are not allowed.

places.name: The Bakasura Footprint
places.blurb: A single footprint in white dust. The only mark left by a non-Architect visitor. Rumored. K-V refuses to confirm. Bakasura smiles, in three of four faces.

continents.blurb: The realm's only soft place. Where decisions about to be made somewhere in the multiverse land as butterflies. Watched closely.

tribes.name: The Twelve Architects
tribes.look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
tribes.customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
tribes.persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

tribes.name: The Ledger Librarians (VOID-II branch)
tribes.look: Cork-bodied. Hollow eyes (carved). Move in silence. Gloves of fresh cork on their hands.
tribes.customs: File. Do not speak. Do not eat. Do not rest. Tend the Library in endless rotating shifts.
tribes.persona: Serene. Devoted. Considered by some scholars to be Ferdinand's own hand-tools.

tribes.name: The White Butterflies
tribes.look: Pure white. Wingspan the size of a palm. No visible head. Landing pattern unpredictable.
tribes.customs: Each butterfly carries a decision about to be finalized somewhere in the multiverse. Landing = decision complete. They do not seem to act with intent — yet they are never wrong.
tribes.persona: Not living in any Scalelist sense. Not dead. Something between.

tribes.name: The Butterfly-Scribes
tribes.look: Small, kneeling, silent. Pale. Ink-stained fingertips. Eyes half-closed.
tribes.customs: Record every butterfly landing. Note time, location on the Wall, and the decision the butterfly represented. File the record in the Library.
tribes.persona: Meditative. Essential. The only VOID-II tribe allowed to speak to Architects during session, and only by passing a written note.

characters.name: K-I — The First Architect
characters.portrait_blurb: Has not moved from the seat since Null learned to think. Robe visibly older than the others. Face, if glimpsed, looks familiar to the viewer — always of someone they once knew.

characters.name: K-II — The Reincarnation Keeper
characters.role: Built Helios Gate City, writes new names on Ferdinand's cork
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall. Carries a cork-quill at all times. Walks the Library's Spine Corridor between sessions. Speaks gently in a voice that feels inherited.

characters.name: K-V — The Current Elder
characters.role: The Architect in authority this cosmic age
characters.portrait_blurb: FORBIDDEN TO NAME. Description redacted. Do not ask. The seat is the description.

characters.name: K-III — The Watcher of the Rim
characters.role: Watches the edges of the universe
characters.portrait_blurb: Cloaked. Eyes visible only. Speaks once per cosmic age, in session. Last said: 'The rim holds.'

characters.name: K-IV — The Silent Architect
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, bird-like. Never makes a sound alone. With K-V, speaks in a whisper so quiet the butterflies stop moving to listen.

characters.name: K-VI through K-XI — The Peripheral Council
characters.portrait_blurb: Six Architects of varying form. They vote. That is all the universe needs of them.

characters.name: K-XII — The Unwritten
characters.role: Broke the Ferdinand Oath. Unwritten from existence.
characters.portrait_blurb: No description possible. Their seat remains. Their books are gone. Their face is a blur in the memory of every Architect except K-I, who remembers everything.

characters.name: Head Librarian Cork
characters.portrait_blurb: Cork-bodied. Seamless. Hollow eye-sockets. Stands at the Library's entrance. Has not moved in three cosmic ages.

characters.name: Chief Scribe Un
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, kneeling, pale. Hand never stops writing. Ink-stained to the elbow. Considered the finest scribe of the current cosmic age.

characters.name: Bakasura (rumored visitor)
characters.role: The only non-Architect confirmed to have left a mark in the Butterfly Garden
characters.portrait_blurb: Three of four faces usually visible. The fourth faces backward. Smiles when asked about the footprint.

fate.threat: The Architects' Seal opens. If the gold-cork disk at the ring's center ever opened, VOID-II would leak into the outer universe. The pre-writing things — the Inscriptions, the first hand — would walk freely. Reality itself would become negotiable by whoever spoke loudest in each moment.
fate.consequence: The end of stable reality. Every realm would become malleable — subject to unwritten versions of itself. The Ledger would be meaningless because the Ledger would change as it was read. Scholars call this possibility 'The First Freedom' — and consider it the most dangerous word in the cosmos.
fate.prophecy_line: The Seal has held because the Oath has held. Do not name the day it would not.
fate.destroyable_by: Only the Architects voting to open it, which would require K-V to cast the tying vote. No K-V has ever done so. The Oath forbids it. Ferdinand himself is believed to be the ultimate safeguard — if the vote approached passage, his cork would harden beyond any chisel.

cultural_notes: VOID-II is less a realm than a LAW. The culture is rule-of-law carried to infinity. Twelve votes, eleven to carry, one to tie, K-V to break. The Library Beyond is the evidence base. The Butterflies are the pulse. The Seal is the boundary. No mortal is meant to set foot here — the realm exists to be reliable, not hospitable.
